controls.title: Start small with bounded, low-risk tasks
controls.guidance: Adopt agentic AI incrementally; prefer tightly scoped pilots before connecting agents to real systems or sensitive data.
controls.matrixscroll_control: Matrix Scroll signs bounded manifests rather than granting agents authority. The SDK records scope, task, reviewer, and policy fields as verifiable evidence.
controls.goes_beyond: Signed boundary reviews are portable and independently auditable; the planned hardware path can bind them to a physical signer.

controls.title: Least privilege and explicit scope
controls.guidance: Give agents only the minimum access they need, for the shortest time required, and limit resources/actions/timeframes.
controls.matrixscroll_control: The evidence manifest includes explicit resource, operation, expiry, and environment fields that are signed before use.
controls.goes_beyond: Offline verifiers can reject unsigned or tampered privilege envelopes without contacting Matrix Scroll.

controls.title: Avoid long-lived credentials
controls.guidance: Use temporary credentials where possible and revoke elevated access when work completes.
controls.matrixscroll_control: Manifests can require short-lived credential metadata and explicitly avoid embedding secrets. Tests enforce redaction-sensitive fields stay unsigned as labels only.
controls.goes_beyond: The signing key itself never appears as an application credential; the planned hardware mode seals it in SSX360/SE050.

controls.title: Human accountability and approval
controls.guidance: Define who owns the agent, approves access, monitors behaviour, reviews incidents, and can stop it.
controls.matrixscroll_control: Signed manifests carry owner, approver, reviewer, and break-glass contact fields as tamper-evident accountability evidence.
controls.goes_beyond: Approvals can be bound to a signature instead of a mutable log entry; the planned hardware path can bind that signature to a physical root of trust.

controls.title: Threat-model the deployment
controls.guidance: Consider misuse, manipulation, prompt injection, unexpected behaviour, and operational failure before deployment.
controls.matrixscroll_control: The security mapping documents threats and maps them to signed provenance, strict verification, and conformance tests.
controls.goes_beyond: Schema/algorithm/device-id tampering are executable tests, not just checklist entries.

controls.title: Secure defaults and input validation
controls.guidance: Use safe configurations, validation, sanitisation, rate limits, and sandboxing appropriate to the integration.
controls.matrixscroll_control: The SDK rejects NaN/Infinity, malformed base64, wrong algorithms, wrong schemas, unsigned manifests, and device-id mismatches by default.
controls.goes_beyond: Canonical JSON and conformance vectors make validation behaviour reproducible across languages.

controls.title: Monitor behaviour and preserve audit evidence
controls.guidance: Maintain visibility into agent operation and look for unusual or unexpected activity across tools and workflows.
controls.matrixscroll_control: Every signed manifest is a portable audit record with device id, public key, timestamp, mode, and immutable signature value.
controls.goes_beyond: Verification works offline and can be run by auditors without trusting the original system.

controls.title: Incident response and kill switch
controls.guidance: Plan for failures, misuse, loss of control, and who can stop an agent.
controls.matrixscroll_control: Evidence manifests include kill-switch and incident-routing fields; failed verification exits non-zero for CI/policy gates.
controls.goes_beyond: Tampered manifests fail closed with deterministic exit code 2.

controls.title: Supply-chain and dependency management
controls.guidance: Understand third-party components, models, tools, integrations, and update exposure.
controls.matrixscroll_control: The repo uses minimal runtime dependencies, Dependabot, CI build verification, and a public spec/conformance suite.
controls.goes_beyond: Third-party implementations can self-test against public vectors before being trusted.

controls.title: Strong authentication and non-repudiation
controls.guidance: Use strong authentication and avoid relying on weak internal safeguards for agent authority.
controls.matrixscroll_control: Ed25519 signatures bind agent evidence to a public key; the planned hardware mode moves the private key into SSX360/SE050.
controls.goes_beyond: The private signing key is separated from agent runtime credentials and can be physically held or removed.

controls.title: Governance and change control
controls.guidance: Integrate agentic AI risk with existing security governance and define ownership before deployment.
controls.matrixscroll_control: Spec, core crypto, vectors, security policy, and CI are CODEOWNERS-protected and tested across OS/Python versions.
controls.goes_beyond: Protocol-impacting changes require both human review and machine conformance checks.

controls.title: Plan for deceptive or manipulated inputs
controls.guidance: Agentic systems inherit prompt-injection/jailbreak risks and need external safeguards, not trust in model behaviour alone.
controls.matrixscroll_control: Matrix Scroll verifies outputs after the model/agent acts; trust is anchored in cryptographic evidence, not model claims.
controls.goes_beyond: A compromised or deceived agent cannot forge a valid signature without the private key.
